steps.title: Identify the contrast signal
steps.content: The passage states the dust has “complex and opposing effects.” The first effect is introduced with “On one hand,”: dust enhances ice clouds. The blank must introduce the opposing effect that follows.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content: ”On the other hand,” is the standard pairing with “On one hand” and directly introduces the opposing effect: dust weakens the storm. The other choices describe time (Previously), illustration (For example), or restatement (In other words)—none match an opposing relationship. That matches choice A — A is correct.
